zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Lindsay of Abercorn and Laird of the Byres於1325年之後出生在蘇格蘭藍納可爾克勞福德時，他的父親David Lindsay Lord of Crawford8700歲，母親Maria De Abernathy8711歲。他於1350年與Christiana Mure of Abercorn在英國蘇格蘭韋斯特 洛迪安阿爾可結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1393年7月1日在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾哈丁頓。

nameMeanings.name: Lindsay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Lindsey in Lincolnshire, England. This is first found in the form Lindissi, apparently a derivative of the British name of Lincoln . To this was later added the Old English element ēg ‘island’, since the place was virtually cut off by the surrounding fenland. The surname was taken to Scotland at an early date and is the name of an important and powerful Scottish family.
Irish: adopted as an equivalent ofvarious Gaelic names (see   Lindsey  ).

variants.name: Linzy
meanings: Altered form of Scottish, Irish, or English   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  . Compare   Lenzy  .

variants.name: Linzey
meanings: English: variant of   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  .

variants.name: Lindsey
meanings: Irish: shortened and Anglicized form of various Gaelic surnames, as for example Ó Loingsigh (see Lynch 1), Mac Giolla Fhionntóg (see McClintock ), and Ó Fhloinn (see Flynn and Lynn ).

variants.name: Lincoln
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of Lincoln, so named from an original British name Lindo- ‘lake’ + Latin colonia ‘settlement, colony’. The place was an important administrative center during the Roman occupation of Britain and in the Middle Ages it was a center for the manufacture of cloth, including the famous ‘Lincoln green’.
African American: adoption of the surname in 1 above, in honor of Abraham Lincoln, the 16th president of the US, who abolished slavery.
History: Abraham Lincoln (1809–65), 16th president of the US, was the son of an illiterate laborer, descended from Samuel Lincoln, who had emigrated from England to MA in 1637.

variants.name: Linsey
meanings: Scottish, Irish, or English: variant of   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  .
Americanized form of German Linse or Swiss German Linsi, see Lins .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Lindsay of Abercorn and Laird of the Byres was born after 1325, in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland, his father, David Lindsay Lord of Crawford, was 8700 and his mother, Maria De Abernathy, was 8711. He married Christiana Mure of Abercorn in 1350, in Abercorn, West Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 1 July 1393, in Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.

Il a épousé Christiana Mure of Abercorn en 1350, à Abercorn, West Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Lindsay of Abercorn and Laird of the Byresが1325年以後にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアクラウフォードで生まれた時，父David Lindsay Lord of Crawfordは8700歳，母Maria De Abernathyは8711歳であった。1350年にイギリススコットランドウエスト ロスアンアバーコルンでChristiana Mure of Abercornと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1393年7月1日にスコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアハッドイントンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Lindsay of Abercorn and Laird of the Byres(이)가 1325년 이후 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 크롤퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 David Lindsay Lord of Crawford(은)는 8700세였고, 그의 어머니 Maria De Abernathy(은)는 8711세였다. 그는 1350년 영국 스코틀랜드 웨스트 로스이언 압르코른에서 Christiana Mure of Abercorn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1393년 7월 1일 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 하딩턴에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Lindsay of Abercorn and Laird of the Byres родился после 1325 г. в местности кравфорд, ланаркшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени David Lindsay Lord of Crawford было 8700 лет, а матери, которую звали Maria De Abernathy, 8711. Он женился на женщине по имени Christiana Mure of Abercorn в 1350 г. в местности абиркорн, вест лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 1 июля 1393 г. в местности хаддингтон, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия.
